zh.lifeSummary: 當William De Clifford於1235年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特時，他的父親Henry de Clifford45歲，母親Maud42歲。他於1268年與Maud Seymour在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1321年在他的家鄉享年86歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Clifford was born in 1235, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry de Clifford, was 45 and his mother, Maud, was 42. He married Maud Seymour in 1268, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1321, in his hometown, at the age of 86, and was buried in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: William De Cliffordが1235年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターで生まれた時，父Henry de Cliffordは45歳，母Maudは42歳であった。1268年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターでMaud Seymourと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1321年に故郷で86歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン ビー シアーエンセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Frampton, Gloucestershire, , England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터
ko.Frampton, Gloucestershire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터
ko.lifeSummary: William De Clifford(이)가 1235년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Clifford(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1268년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 Maud Seymour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1321년 그의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William De Clifford nasceu em 1235, em Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry de Clifford, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Maud, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Seymour em 1268, em Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1321, em sua cidade natal, com 86 anos, e foi sepultado em Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William De Clifford родился в 1235 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry de Clifford было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Seymour в 1268 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1321 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 86 лет и был похоронен в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William De Clifford nació en 1235, en Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry de Clifford, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Maud, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Maud Seymour en 1268, en Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1321, en su pueblo, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultado en Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
